page.heroTitle: No Upload. No AI. No Exceptions.
page.heroSubtext: PatentLint runs entirely inside your browser. Your patent draft never leaves your device. Analysis is local; results are yours. No account. No telemetry. No personal data.
page.compareDisclaimer: Comparison reflects general architecture patterns. Individual products may vary. "Word Add-in Tools" and "Cloud-Based Tools" are generic categories, not specific products.

q1.question: Does my file leave my device?
q1.addin: File stays local, but add-in may phone home.

q2.question: Do I need an account?
q2.patentlint: No account required. PatentLint collects no personal data.
q2.addin: Usually requires a license or account.

q4.question: Can I verify the privacy claim?
q4.patentlint: Yes. Open DevTools → Network, or go airplane mode.
q4.cloud: No. Data is on their servers.

q5.question: What platforms are supported?
q5.patentlint: Any device with a modern browser.

q6.question: What does it cost?
q6.patentlint: Free — no subscription, no usage limits.

q7.question: Is my patent text used for AI training?
q7.patentlint: Never. All checks are deterministic rule-based pattern matching.
q7.addin: Depends on vendor — some send telemetry.
q7.cloud: Often yes. Terms of service commonly reserve such rights.

tech.architecture: PatentLint uses a client-side-only architecture: its Python analysis engine compiles to WebAssembly via Pyodide and runs entirely inside your browser's sandboxed JavaScript environment. No server-side processing occurs for document analysis.
tech.network: After the initial page load (which loads the analysis engine), zero network requests are made during analysis. Verify this in your browser's DevTools Network tab, or simply turn on airplane mode.
tech.pdf: PDF reports are generated entirely in your browser using pdfmake. Your document content never leaves your device, not even for the report.
tech.ai: PatentLint does not use AI, machine learning, or large language models. All checks are deterministic, rule-based pattern matching against patent office guidelines. Your text is never sent to any AI service.
tech.telemetry: PatentLint includes no analytics scripts, tracking pixels, cookies, or third-party tracking integrations. No usage data is collected or transmitted automatically.
tech.selfHosted: On-premises deployment is available on private inquiry. Compliance with any specific regulatory regime remains the organization's own determination.

about.productTagline: A first-of-its-kind patent draft checker that runs offline in your browser.
about.productDesc1: PatentLint runs entirely on your own device, catching issues for you before the examiner does. The webpage is just the entry point. Once loaded, PatentLint runs on your device like desktop software — you can disconnect from the internet and keep using it. Drop a U.S., Chinese, or Taiwanese patent draft for an instant compliance report and grade on formal requirements. No uploads, no cloud processing, no AI.
about.productDesc2: PatentLint checks drafting rules from the USPTO (U.S.), CNIPA (China), and TIPO (Taiwan), detecting issues commonly flagged by examination standards, such as missing antecedent basis, incorrect claim structure, and restrictive wording.
about.usptoSubtitle: USPTO Patent Center validates filing format. PatentLint checks the draft itself.

about.builderPain: Every patent professional knows the drill. Antecedent basis. Claim terms missing from the spec. Figures declared in the drawings description but never referenced in the body. Dependency chains that quietly become circular. None of it is hard. All of it is tedious. All of it is easy to miss.
about.builderDesc1: PatentLint was built by a patent engineer who spent over a decade inside that workflow, reviewing thousands of drafts and watching the same categories of errors slip through again and again. Existing options were either Windows-only desktop installs or cloud services that required uploading a client's unfiled application to someone else's server. One locks you to a specific OS. The other sends the draft out of your firm before it's even on file.
about.builderDesc2: The analysis engine is written in Python and compiles to WebAssembly. It runs entirely in the browser, on a laptop, tablet, or phone, with nothing uploaded and nothing logged. That means it works on an airplane, on a locked-down firm machine, on anything a client hasn't signed off on yet.
about.builderSister: Its companion project, the Agentic Patent Analyst, runs in-house fine-tuned LLM agents that read Office Actions, weigh each examiner rejection, and draft argument or amendment strategies.
about.tableFootnote: As of April 2026; both tools may continue to evolve.
about.twTableFootnote: TIPO coverage audited against 專利申請文件輔助偵錯系統 — 功能介紹與使用指南 (2023.5.30 版). TIPO 偵錯系統 supports utility-model filings only; PatentLint supports both utility models and inventions. Both tools may continue to evolve.

details.firstGapParagraph: First gap at paragraph {{position}}. Renumber to restore sequential order per MPEP § 608.01(p).
details.firstGapClaim: First gap at claim {{position}}. Renumber claims sequentially per MPEP § 608.01(j).
details.missingSections: Missing: {{list}}. Add these sections before filing.
details.nounMismatch: Dependent '{{dependent}}' vs independent '{{independent}}'. A dependent claim must be of the same statutory category as its parent (e.g., both apparatus or both method). Correct the preamble noun.
details.seeSection112: See § 112 Analysis below for per-claim detail.
details.specSupportUnsupported: The {{count}} claim term(s) shown here were not found in the specification body. Each claim term should have written-description support.
details.restrictiveWordingSpec: Paragraphs: {{list}}. These terms may be treated as indefinite or limiting under MPEP § 2173.05(b). Review each flagged paragraph and consider whether the term unduly limits scope or introduces indefiniteness.
details.paragraphEnding: Paragraphs: {{list}}. Each specification paragraph must end with a period per MPEP § 608.01(p).
details.sequenceListingFix: Add a "Statement Regarding Sequence Listing" section per 37 C.F.R. § 1.821–1.825.
details.crossReferenceCitations: {{text}}. Verify serial numbers are correct and the priority chain is complete (continuations, CIPs, provisionals, divisionals).
details.priorArtCitations: {{text}}. Verify patent/publication numbers are accurate. Characterizations of prior art may be treated as admissions during prosecution.
details.optionalSectionGuidance: Include this section if applicable to your application.
details.restrictiveAbsolutesClaims: Claims: {{list}}. Absolute terms such as "must," "essential," "always," "never" can narrow claim scope unnecessarily and may create § 112(b) indefiniteness when the specification doesn't actually require the absoluteness asserted. Per MPEP § 2173.01, review each flagged term and rephrase if the restriction is not supported.
details.indefiniteWordingClaims: Claims: {{list}}. Relative or indefinite terms such as "substantially," "approximately," "generally," "may," "preferably," "or the like" leave claim scope unclear. Per MPEP § 2173.05(b), review each flagged term and replace with precise limitations or remove if not necessary.
details.multipleDependentClaims: Claims: {{list}}. Multiple-dependent claims incur additional fees and must not depend on other multiple-dependent claims (35 U.S.C. § 112(e)). Verify this is intentional.
details.chainedMultiDepClaims: Claims: {{list}}. Under 35 U.S.C. § 112(e) (and MPEP § 608.01(n)), a multi-dependent claim cannot depend on another multi-dependent claim. Rewrite the flagged claim(s) to either depend on a single claim or to depend directly on independent claims (not via another multi-dep).
details.selfDependentClaims: Claims: {{list}}. A claim cannot depend on itself. Correct the dependency reference.
details.meansFunctionClaims: Claims: {{list}}. Under § 112(f), claim scope is limited to the corresponding structure disclosed in the specification and equivalents. Verify that the specification describes sufficient structure for each means-plus-function limitation.
details.antecedentBasisTerms: {{count}} potential antecedent basis issues across {{claims}} claims. Each definite reference ("the X" or "said X") must have a prior indefinite introduction ("a X" or "an X"). Missing antecedent basis may result in a § 112(b) indefiniteness rejection.
details.preambleNounMismatch: Claim {{claim}} depends on claim {{parent}}. Preamble noun differs: "{{dependent}}" vs parent "{{independent}}". May be acceptable if they refer to the same entity. Verify consistency.
details.preambleNounMismatchSummary: Claim {{rootId}} uses parent noun '{{parent}}'; dependent claim(s) {{depClaims}} use '{{dependent}}'. Verify whether these refer to the same entity or align the preamble nouns.
details.preambleIndefiniteArticle: Claim {{claim}} depends on claim {{parent}}. The dependent preamble uses "{{article}} {{noun}}" — it should be "The {{noun}}" to refer back to the independent claim entity.
details.preambleParseUnclear: Claims: {{claims}}. The walker couldn't parse these dependent-claim preambles. MPEP § 608.01(n) compliant forms include: 'The X of claim N', 'The X according to claim N', 'The X as in claim N', 'The X as claimed in claim N', 'The X as recited in claim N', 'The X as set forth in claim N'. Verify manually, or report a bug if the preamble uses a standard MPEP form we haven't recognized.
details.singleFigureFix: Replace all instances of "FIG. 1" with "The Figure" or "the drawing" per MPEP § 608.02.
details.drawingsPriorArt: Figures depicting prior art must be designated with the legend "Prior Art" per MPEP § 608.02(g). Verify each prior art figure is correctly labeled.
details.figuresSequentialFix: Renumber figures in ascending order per MPEP § 608.02.
details.orphanedBriefFigures: Figures: {{list}}. Either add a reference to these figures in the Detailed Description or remove them from the Brief Description.
details.orphanedDetailedFigures: Figures: {{list}}. Either add a description for these figures in the Brief Description of Drawings or remove the reference from the Detailed Description.
details.legalPhraseologyAbstract: Per MPEP § 608.01(b), the abstract should avoid "the form and legal phraseology often used in patent claims" — the manual names "means" and "said," and practitioner guidance extends the class to transitional phrases ("comprising," "comprises," etc.), claim modifiers ("wherein"), and archaic claim-style pronouns ("thereof," "the same"). Rephrase the flagged terms.
details.meritLanguageAbstract: Per MPEP § 608.01(b), the abstract should not refer to purported merits of the invention — avoid evaluative adjectives ("novel," "innovative," "unique," "important," "significant") and self-referential phrasing ("the present invention"). Rephrase the flagged terms.
details.abstractStructureFix: The abstract must be a single paragraph ending with a period, exclamation point, or question mark, and must appear as the last section of the specification per MPEP § 608.01(b).
details.abstractImpliedPhrasesFix: Per MPEP § 608.01(b), openers like "This disclosure provides..." or "A method is provided..." should be rewritten to describe the invention directly (e.g., "A method that...").
details.abstractWordCountFix: The abstract must be between 50 and 150 words per MPEP § 608.01(b). Revise to meet this range.

cn.requiredSections: A complete CNIPA filing requires 摘要, 说明书 (技术领域, 背景技术, 发明内容, 具体实施方式), and 权利要求书 per 专利法 §26 第1款. Missing: {{sections}}.
cn.sectionOrdering: Prescribed order: 技术领域 → 背景技术 → 发明内容 → 附图说明 → 具体实施方式. Reorder sections to match.
cn.paragraphNumberingXml: Per 审查指南, paragraph numbers must be sequential and zero-padded (0001, 0002, ...). Fix the numbering sequence.
cn.paragraphNumberingDocx: Per CNIPA guidelines, specifications must not contain paragraph numbering. The WORD转XML编辑器 adds numbering automatically during conversion. Remove all manually added paragraph numbers.
cn.paragraphEnding: Per 审查指南, specification paragraphs must end with Chinese sentence-final punctuation (。, ！, or ？). Check the flagged paragraphs.
cn.figureRefConsistency: Per 审查指南, figures described in 附图说明 should be referenced in 具体实施方式 and vice versa. Verify the flagged references are intentional.
cn.patentTypeTerminology: Per 审查指南, invention applications should use 本发明 (not 本实用新型), and utility model applications vice versa. Found: {{term}}. Verify the terminology matches your filing type.
cn.titleLength: Per 审查指南 第一部分第一章, the title (发明名称) must not exceed 25 Chinese characters ({{count}} found). Shorten the title.
cn.titleContent: Per 审查指南, the title must not contain trademarks, model numbers, personal names, or commercial/advertising language. Remove the prohibited content.
cn.specClaimReference: Per 专利法实施细则 §20, the specification must not use language such as '如权利要求……所述的……' to reference claims. Remove claim references from the specification.
cn.claimsSequential: Per 审查指南, claims must be numbered sequentially starting from 1. Fix the numbering.
cn.dependencyFormat: Per 专利法实施细则 §25, dependent claims must use the format '如权利要求N所述的……' to reference parent claims. Fix the flagged claims.
cn.selfDependent: Per 专利法实施细则 §25, a claim cannot depend on itself. Correct the dependency reference.
cn.forwardDependency: Per 专利法实施细则 §25, dependent claims must only reference preceding claims. Change the dependency to reference an earlier claim.
cn.singleSentence: Per 审查指南 第二部分第二章, each claim must be a single sentence ending with one 。. Remove mid-claim periods or restructure into separate claims.
cn.refNumeralParens: Per 审查指南, reference numerals in claims should be enclosed in parentheses, e.g., (101) not 101. Verify the flagged claims.
cn.subjectConsistency: Per 审查指南 第二部分第二章, the subject matter (主题名称) of a dependent claim should match its parent claim's subject matter. Recognized preamble forms: 如权利要求N所述[的]X, 如权利要求N所记载[的]X, 如权利要求N[的]X. Verify the flagged claims.
cn.subjectConsistencyParseUnclear: These dependent claims use a preamble form the walker doesn't recognize. Accepted forms per 专利法实施细则 §25 include: 如权利要求N所述[的]X, 如权利要求N所记载[的]X, 如权利要求N[的]X. Verify manually, or report a bug if your preamble is CNIPA-compliant.
cn.transitionPhrase: Per 审查指南, independent claims should contain a characterizing transition such as '其特征在于' to delineate known elements from novel features. Verify the flagged claims.
cn.twTerminology: Taiwan (TIPO) patent terminology detected in CNIPA filing. CNIPA uses '权利要求' for claims. Replace all flagged instances.
cn.claimsSpecReference: Per 审查指南 第二部分第二章, claims must not reference the specification or drawings to define their scope. Claims must be self-contained — remove the flagged references.
cn.multiMultiDep: Per 专利法实施细则 §25, a multiple-dependent claim must not serve as the basis for another multiple-dependent claim. Restructure the dependency chain.
cn.dependentOrdering: Per 审查指南 第二部分第二章, all claims depending on an independent claim should appear consecutively before the next independent claim. Reorder claims.
cn.connectionRelationships: Components flagged: {{sample_names}} ({{component_count}} total). Add a connection verb (e.g., 连接, 设置于, 耦接) describing how these components are structurally arranged.
cn.abstractCharCount: Per 专利法实施细则 §26, the abstract must not exceed 300 Chinese characters. Shorten the abstract.
cn.abstractTitleMatch: Per 审查指南, the abstract should be consistent with the title (发明名称). Verify the abstract reflects the invention title.
cn.commercialLanguage: Per 专利法实施细则 §26, the abstract must not contain commercial advertising language (商业性宣传用语). Remove the flagged terms.
cn.antecedentBasisTerms: {{issue_count}} term(s) across {{claim_count}} claim(s) may lack antecedent basis. See the "Antecedent Basis Review" section below for the full per-term breakdown.
cn.specSupportTerms: {{issue_count}} term(s) across {{claim_count}} claim(s) may lack specification support. See the "Specification Support Review" section below for the full per-term breakdown.
cn.titleMissing: Per CNIPA 审查指南 第一部分第一章, the specification must contain a 发明名称 (title of invention). Add a title to the specification header.

figureRefInconsistency.onlyDrawings: Only cited in 附图说明: {{figs}}
figureRefInconsistency.onlyEmbodiment: Only cited in 具体实施方式: {{figs}}

pass.multiMultiDep: Per 专利法实施细则 §25, a multiple-dependent claim may not serve as a basis for another multiple-dependent claim — chained multi-multi references make scope indeterminate.
pass.selfDependent: Per 专利法实施细则 §25, a claim may not depend on itself; dependent claims must reference an earlier claim.
pass.forwardDependency: Per 专利法实施细则 §25, a dependent claim may only reference earlier-numbered claims, not later ones.
pass.singleSentence: Per 审查指南 第二部分第二章, each claim must be a single sentence ending in 。 to keep scope unambiguous.
pass.transitionPhrase: Per 审查指南, an independent claim should contain a two-part transition (e.g. 其特征在于) separating the preamble (prior art) from the characterizing part (novel features).

cn.independentPreamble: 审查指南 第二部分第二章 §3.1.1 uses 一种X as the canonical form; 专利法实施细则 §24 requires the preamble to state the 主题名称 (subject-matter name) but does not literally mandate 一种. Consider rewriting to the 一种X form.

tw.requiredSections: A complete TIPO filing requires 摘要, 說明書 (技術領域, 先前技術, 發明內容/新型內容, 實施方式), and 申請專利範圍 per 專利法 §25 第1項. When drawings are present, 圖式簡單說明 and 符號說明 are also required per 專利法施行細則 §17. Missing: {{sections}}.
tw.sectionOrdering: 專利法施行細則 §17 prescribes: 技術領域 → 先前技術 → 發明內容/新型內容 → 圖式簡單說明 → 實施方式 → 符號說明. Reorder to match.
tw.paragraphNumbering: When present, paragraph numbers must use 【NNNN】 format with sequential four-digit numbers. Numbering is optional per 專利法施行細則 §17.
tw.paragraphEnding: Specification paragraphs should end with Chinese punctuation (。！？). Review flagged paragraphs.
tw.figureRefConsistency: Per 專利審查基準, figures described in 圖式簡單說明 should be referenced in 實施方式 and vice versa. Verify the flagged references are intentional.
tw.patentTypeTerminology: Invention patents (發明) should use 本發明, not 本新型. Utility models (新型) should use 本新型, not 本發明. Found: {{term}}.
tw.title: 發明名稱/新型名稱 should be concise with no trademarks, model numbers, or irrelevant text.
tw.claimReference: Per 專利法施行細則 §17, the specification must not reference specific claims (e.g., 如請求項N所述). Found: {{detail}}.
tw.symbolTablePresence: Per 專利法施行細則 §17, when 圖式簡單說明 is present, 符號說明 must also be included. Add a 符號說明 section listing reference numerals and component names.
tw.symbolTableConsistency: Per 專利審查基準, 符號說明 entries should match reference numerals used in 實施方式. Verify completeness.
tw.claimsSequential: Per 專利審查基準, claim numbers must be sequential starting from 1.
tw.dependencyFormat: Per 專利法施行細則 §18, dependencies must use a recognized format (如請求項N所述之, 如請求項N之, etc.). Review flagged claims.
tw.selfDependent: Per 專利法施行細則 §18, a claim cannot depend on itself. Self-dependent: {{claims}}.
tw.circularDependency: Per 專利法施行細則 §18, claims must not form circular dependency chains. Circular chain: 請求項 {{claims}}.
tw.forwardDependency: Per 專利法施行細則 §18, dependent claims must only reference preceding claims. Forward references: {{claims}}.
tw.singleSentence: Per 專利法施行細則 §18, each claim must be one sentence with exactly one 。 at the end. Review flagged claims.
tw.refNumeralParens: Per 專利法施行細則 §19, reference numerals in claims should be enclosed in parentheses, e.g., (101). Review flagged claims.
tw.subjectConsistency: Dependent claim subject matter should match the parent claim's subject matter. Recognized preamble forms: 如請求項N所述[之的]X, 如請求項N所記載[之的]X, 如請求項N[之的]X. Review flagged claims.
tw.subjectConsistencyParseUnclear: These dependent claims use a preamble form the walker doesn't recognize. Accepted forms per 專利法施行細則 §18 include: 如請求項N所述[之的]X, 如請求項N所記載[之的]X, 如請求項N[之的]X. Verify manually, or report a bug if your preamble is TIPO-compliant.
tw.transitionPhrase: Per 專利法施行細則 §20, independent claims should contain a transitional phrase such as 其特徵在於 or 其改良在於. Review flagged claims.
tw.cnTerminology: Found simplified Chinese patent terminology: {{detail}}. TW documents should use TIPO terms (請求項, 說明書, 先前技術, 實施方式, etc.), not CNIPA terms (权利要求, 说明书, 背景技术, 具体实施方式, etc.).
tw.specDrawingRef: Per 專利法施行細則 §19, claims must not reference the specification or drawings (e.g., 如說明書所述, 如圖所示). Found: {{detail}}.
tw.multiDepOnMultiDep: Per 專利法施行細則 §18, a multi-dependent claim cannot directly or indirectly depend on another multi-dependent claim. Flagged: {{claims}}.
tw.multiDepAlternative: Per 專利法施行細則 §18, multi-dependent claims must use alternative form (或/任一項). Flagged: {{claims}}.
tw.titleSubjectMatch: Per TIPO 專利審查基準, the title should match the subject of independent claims. Review flagged claims.

symbolTableConsistency.missingFromTable: Numerals in claims undefined in 符號說明: {{numerals_with_locations}}

tw.antecedentBasis: 該/所述/前述 + noun should have a matching prior introduction in the same claim or a parent claim. The 一 + noun form is the canonical convention; alternatives (一個 / 至少一 / 複數個 / 多個 + noun) are also accepted. Regex-based detection is approximate.
tw.abstractCharCount: Per 專利法施行細則 §21, the abstract should be within 250 characters. TIPO may not reject on length alone, but review for conciseness.
tw.abstractTitleMatch: Per 專利審查基準, the abstract should reference the 發明名稱/新型名稱. Verify the abstract reflects the title. Title: {{detail}}.
tw.commercialLanguage: Per 專利法施行細則 §21, the abstract must not contain commercial advertising language (商業性宣傳用語). Found: {{terms}}.
tw.representativeDrawing: Per 專利法施行細則 §21, when drawings exist, a 代表圖 should be designated. Review whether a representative drawing designation is needed.

symbolVsRepDrawing.nameMismatch: {{numeral}}: representative drawing symbol description has "{{rep_name}}", 符號說明 has "{{table_name}}"
symbolVsRepDrawing.description: Symbols listed in the representative drawing symbol description should match the definitions in 符號說明. Verify the flagged items.

tw.bracketFormat: Per 專利法施行細則 §17, section headers should use full-width brackets 【】. Found: {{detail}}.
tw.connectionRelationships: Components flagged: {{sample_names}} ({{component_count}} total). Add a connection verb (e.g., 連接, 設置於, 耦接) describing how these components are structurally arranged.

tw.specSupportTerms: {{issue_count}} term(s) across {{claim_count}} claim(s) may lack specification support. See the Specification Support Review section below for the full per-term breakdown.
tw.titleMissing: Per TIPO 專利審查基準, the specification must contain a 發明名稱/新型名稱 (title of invention or utility model). Add one to the specification header.

tw.singleSentenceMissingPeriod: Per 專利法施行細則 §18, each claim must end with a single 。. Add the closing period to the flagged claims.
tw.singleSentenceMultiSentence: Per 專利法施行細則 §18, each claim must be one sentence with exactly one 。 at the end. Remove mid-claim 。 or restructure the flagged claims.
tw.independentPreamble: TIPO 偵錯系統 flags this as a convention error. 專利法施行細則 §18 + 專利審查基準 require the preamble to state the 標的名稱 (subject-matter name) but do not literally mandate 「一種」. Consider rewriting to the 一種X form if that matches the invention.
tw.indigenousTerms: Terms: {{terms}}. Per 原住民族傳統智慧創作保護條例, verify the filing does not conflict with protected traditional creations.

Without this qualifier, the claim covers transitory signals (e.g., carrier waves), which are not patent-eligible subject matter under 35 U.S.C. § 101. Add 'non-transitory' before the medium term.
claims.markushOpenTransition: Claim {{claimNumber}}: Markush group uses open-ended '{{transition}}' instead of 'consisting of'
claims.markushOpenTransitionDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} contains a Markush group using '{{transition}}' instead of the required 'consisting of'. Markush groups must use closed transitional language per MPEP § 2117. Using open-ended language may result in an improper Markush grouping rejection.
claims.omnibusClaim: Claim {{claimNumber}} appears to be an omnibus claim
claims.omnibusClaimDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} references the description or drawings without reciting specific technical features. Omnibus claims are categorically rejected under 35 U.S.C. § 112(b) in U.S. utility patents (MPEP § 2173.05(r)). Rewrite the claim to recite specific structural or method limitations.
claims.specialFormatsPass: No special claim format issues detected.
claims.missingPeriod: Claim {{claimNumber}} does not end with a period.
claims.missingPeriodDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} is missing its final period. Every claim must end with a single period per MPEP § 608.01(m).
claims.extraPeriod: Claim {{claimNumber}} contains extra or misplaced periods.
claims.extraPeriodDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} has periods in unexpected positions. A claim should contain only one period at the very end per MPEP § 608.01(m).
claims.whereinComma: Claim {{claimNumber}}: review comma usage before 'wherein' clause.
claims.whereinCommaDetails: Claim {{claimNumber}} may have incorrect comma placement around a 'wherein' clause. Review punctuation per MPEP § 608.01(m).

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy describes how patentlint.com (the "Site") handles data when you visit. The PatentLint application collects nothing about you — no analytics, no trackers, no cookies. The only data flow is the standard web request metadata our hosting provider (Cloudflare Pages) sees on every visit, as it does for any website. This Policy makes that distinction explicit.

s1.title: Introduction
s1.p1: patentlint.com is a hosted web application operated by Christopher Chen. It runs PatentLint, a patent drafting checker that analyzes your file entirely in your browser using WebAssembly (Pyodide). The Site is served as static assets from Cloudflare Pages, with a single Cloudflare Pages Function for the voluntary error-report endpoint described in § 6. There is no analysis server and no database. Your file content is never transmitted off your device.
s1.p2: This Privacy Policy applies to your interactions with the Site. It does not cover the PatentLint Software when you obtain the source code separately — that's covered by the Software license (PolyForm-Strict-1.0.0).

s2.title: Your patent drafts
s2.p1: Patent drafts you load into the Site never actually leave your device — the file stays in the browser tab and is processed there. Analysis runs on a Pyodide / WebAssembly engine in that tab. There is no server-side analysis path on patentlint.com; the Site is served as static assets, with a single voluntary error-report endpoint (§ 6) that runs only on explicit user submission.
s2.never1: Receive or transmit the content of your patent drafts.
s2.never2: Store filenames, claim text, specification body, or any patent-specific data.
s2.never3: Send draft content to any third party (including AI providers, OCR services, or document processors).
s2.verify: You can verify this in airplane mode — see the <1>Security</1> page for the live demonstration.
s2.p3: Scope of processing. PatentLint does not act as a "data controller" or "data processor" within the meaning of GDPR Art. 4(7)–(8), nor as a "business" within the meaning of CCPA/CPRA, with respect to the patent draft content you analyze. That content is processed entirely within your browser tab and is never transmitted to any PatentLint server. We act as a controller only with respect to (i) the voluntary diagnostic report you submit via the "Send" button, if you choose to submit one, and (ii) standard hosting metadata logged by our static-asset provider (see § 3).

s3.title: Cloudflare-edge hosting metadata
s3.p1: The Site is hosted on Cloudflare Pages (a static-asset CDN). When your browser fetches the Site, Cloudflare's edge network may log standard web request metadata as part of normal hosting operations:
s3.log1: Your IP address (used by Cloudflare for routing and DDoS protection).
s3.log3: The URL path you requested and the HTTP response status.
s3.p2: This is the same metadata any web host receives — we do not augment it, enrich it, link it to identity, or use it for any analytics. Cloudflare retains its edge logs per its own published retention policy.
s3.p3: Under GDPR Art. 4(7) we are technically a data controller for this Cloudflare-edge processing. We don't access these logs in the ordinary course of operating the Site; we'd only consult them if investigating a security incident.

s4.title: No analytics or tracking
s4.p1: The Site contains no analytics scripts, tracking pixels, fingerprinting code, or third-party advertising integrations. Specifically:
s4.no1: No Google Analytics, Plausible, Umami, or equivalent.
s4.no2: No Meta Pixel, LinkedIn Insight, or other advertising trackers.
s4.no3: No session replay, heatmap, or behavioral analytics.
s4.p2: If you enable a network monitor in your browser's developer tools, you'll see the Site issues no analytics requests during use. This is verifiable in airplane mode after the initial page load.

s5.title: Cookies and local storage
s5.p1: We do not set cookies. The Site is fully usable with cookies disabled.
s5.p2: Your browser may use localStorage to remember small preferences (selected language, theme choice, dismissal state for in-app banners). This data stays on your device and is never transmitted. Clearing your browser storage clears these preferences.

s6.title: Voluntary feedback
s6.p1: Each finding has a "Report" button. Clicking it opens a dialog that previews exactly what will be sent — nothing is hidden. The dialog also offers a "Send by email" alternative addressed to kwisschen@gmail.com if you would rather write more context yourself.
s6.p2: The anonymous payload contains counts, the specific term or phrase the rule flagged, a short surrounding excerpt, category labels (which check, which rule, which detection branch), and a few structural fingerprints — capped at five findings per report. It does not contain your full claim text, full paragraphs, file names, file metadata, your email, your name, or your IP.
s6.p3: When you click "Send anonymously," your browser POSTs the payload over HTTPS to a Cloudflare Pages Function on patentlint.com that creates an issue in a private GitHub Issues tracker only the maintainer can read. Your IP and browser headers are not forwarded to GitHub. Reports cannot be linked back to your identity.
s6.p4: If you use the email alternative instead, we receive whatever you write. To send pseudonymously, use an email-aliasing service (Apple Hide My Email, Gmail aliases, Firefox Relay, ProtonMail aliases); we do not correlate alias addresses with any other data. We use feedback only to fix bugs and reply to you, and we do not share it with third parties.

s7.title: Third-party services
s7.p1: PatentLint relies on the following third-party services to operate. Each is listed for transparency:
s7.cloudflare: Hosts the static Site assets and the Pyodide wheel. Receives the standard web request metadata described in § 3.
s7.pyodide: On first visit, your browser fetches the Pyodide WebAssembly runtime from the Pyodide CDN (jsdelivr / cloudflare). After that, the runtime is cached locally and works offline.
s7.fonts: If you export a localized PDF in zh-TW / zh-CN / ja / ko, your browser fetches the corresponding Noto Sans CJK font from Google Fonts at PDF-export time. The font is cached after first fetch. English-locale PDFs do not fetch any font.
s7.github: Anonymous reports submitted via the "Send" button in the report dialog are forwarded by a Cloudflare Pages Function to a private GitHub Issues tracker (kwisschen/Patent-Lint) only the maintainer can access. GitHub receives the de-identified payload only — no IP, no email, no browser headers — and stores issues under its own privacy policy.
s7.p2: We do not use any other third-party services. No CRM, no email-marketing platform, no support ticketing system, no third-party logging.

s8.title: Your rights
s8.p1: Under GDPR, CCPA, and similar privacy regimes, you have rights regarding your personal data. To the extent these rights apply to data we process:
s8.access: You may ask what personal data we hold about you. We do not store user data in the ordinary course of operating the Site (see § 2 and § 4); requests will typically confirm we have nothing on file beyond any voluntary email correspondence you have sent us.
s8.deletion: You may ask us to delete personal data we hold about you. As with access requests, there is typically nothing to delete; voluntary email correspondence is the rare exception.
s8.portability: You may ask for a copy of personal data we hold about you in a portable format.
s8.object: You may object to our processing or withdraw any consent you previously gave.
s8.exercise: Exercise these rights by emailing <1>kwisschen@gmail.com</1>. We respond within 30 days. Because the PatentLint application collects nothing and we do not store usage data, most requests will simply confirm we have nothing on file to access, delete, or export.

s9.title: Children
s9.p1: patentlint.com is intended for patent practitioners and is not directed at children under 16. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe a child has interacted with the Site in a way that involved personal data collection, please contact us so we can investigate.

s10.title: Changes to this Policy
s10.p1: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Material changes will be announced via a banner on the Site for at least 14 days before taking effect. The "Last updated" date at the top of this page reflects the current version.

s11.title: Contact
s11.p1: For questions about this Privacy Policy or to exercise the rights described in § 8:
s11.name: Christopher Chen

trust.line: This grade only reflects PatentLint's check results. It is not a judgment on the draft's substantive content.

section.title: title
section.notApplicableTooltip: No drawings detected — section not graded

page.title: How PatentLint scores your draft
page.subtitle: A deterministic rubric — no AI judgment, no machine learning.
page.weightsIntro: Every check we run rolls into one of these five sections. Weights are fixed across US, CN, and TW filings.
page.gateIntro: Statutory blockers headline the grade. One FIX caps the grade at A-, regardless of how clean the rest of the draft is. This matches partner-triage reality — the question is always "any blockers, then how clean?"
page.conditionalIntro: Two scenarios shape the Drawings section. First — when no drawings are detected (chemistry, methods, business processes), the section is marked N/A and its 10% weight redistributes proportionally across the other four sections; legitimately drawing-free drafts incur no penalty. Second — when drawings exist (FIG. references found in the body) but the Brief Description of Drawings section is missing, the resulting required-section FIX lands in the Drawings rubric so the section pill reflects the actual gap.
page.completenessIntro: When a universally-required section (title, claims, specification body, or abstract) is structurally empty, we do not emit a grade. An A grade on an empty draft would be misleading.
page.trustIntro: Every input to your grade is a FIX/REVIEW/PASS status set by a deterministic checker. Every output is a pure arithmetic aggregation. The same draft always gets the same grade. PatentLint never uploads your draft anywhere — analysis runs entirely in your browser.
page.disclaimerTitle: What an A grade does and does not mean
page.disclaimerIntro: An A grade means PatentLint's rule catalog found no issues. It does not mean your draft is filing-ready. Substantive review by a registered practitioner is still recommended.
page.reportIntro: No checker is perfect. Walker-based checks (antecedent basis, specification support, figure references) carry a small false-positive risk; with grading now in place, a wrongly-flagged FIX can cost the draft a letter rank. The Report button on each triage item opens a dialog that previews exactly what will be sent — counts, the flagged term, a short surrounding excerpt, and category labels, capped at five findings — so we can pinpoint the bug from a de-identified payload.

endpointDesc.report: Anonymous error report — diagnostic trail only, no draft content.
